channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Lindsey Vonn races into 14th in a super-G to mark her return to World Cup skiing at age 40
item.description: ST. MORITZ, Switzerland (AP) — Lindsey Vonn raced into 14th position in a super-G on Saturday to mark her return to World Cup skiing at age 40. Vonn finished 1.18 seconds behind leader Cornelia Huetter. It’s the American standout’s first World Cup race after nearly six years of retirement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UCSD basketball heating up, Tritons on a tear after historic upset
item.description: Fresh after pulling off what might be one of the biggest wins in program history, UCSD men's basketball has no plans on slowing down.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Joel Embiid returns from sinus fracture to play against Charlotte
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Joel Embiid started for the Philadelphia 76ers against Charlotte on Friday night after missing a game because of a sinus fracture. Embiid was introduced to a roaring ovation and wore a protective “ Phantom of the Opera ” mask in his return.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Security guards tackle two women to the ground during Knicks-Timberwolves game after they ran onto court
item.description: Two NBA fans attempted to storm the court Thursday night during the New York Knicks' win over the Minnesota Timberwolves, but the two women were tackled to the ground by security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former NFL coach Jon Gruden says pass interference is 'biggest concern,' suggests cap on penalty yardage
item.description: The NFL's pass interference rule often sparks controversy, and a Super Bowl-winning coach wants the league to impose penalty yardage like college football.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jalen Hurts fined for wearing mismatched cleats during Eagles' win over Steelers, AP source says
item.description: A person familiar with the decision says Philadelphia Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts was fined $5,628 by the NFL for wearing mismatched cleats that were not “constitutional team colors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Christian Walker agrees to $60 million, 3-year contract with Astros, AP source says
item.description: A a person familiar with the negotiations tells The Associated Press that Gold Glove first baseman Christian Walker and the Houston Astros have agreed to a $60 million, three-year contract.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Penn State and SMU carry the weight of history into their CFP debuts. They're both trying to shed it
item.description: DaQuan Jones remembers the chaos. The uncertainty. The sanctions. The aftermath. How could he not? He and the rest of his Penn State teammates — those that stuck around anyway — lived through it.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bulls beat Celtics 117-108 in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Celtics forward Jaylen Brown took issue with back-to-back technical fouls called on coach Joe Mazzulla and himself late in the fourth quarter of Boston’s 117-108 loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taking advantage of an obscure rule, Chargers' Dicker makes NFL's first fair-catch kick in 48 years
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Of all the rules in football, Jim Harbaugh considers the obscure fair-catch kick to be his favorite. The Los Angeles Chargers coach had the opportunity to try one Thursday night for only the second time in his career.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chargers take advantage of obscure NFL rule with free kick field goal last successful in 1976
item.description: The Los Angeles Chargers pulled off the first successful free kick field goal since 1976, which was also achieved by the Chargers when they played in San diego.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An 'embarrassing' night for Stephen Curry and the Warriors, who fall by 51 at Memphis
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Stephen Curry had a game like none other in his career. It was part of an awful night for the Golden State Warriors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brock Purdy gets his offensive line new cars for Christmas
item.description: (KRON) -- It's the most wonderful time of the year -- especially if you're a San Francisco 49ers offensive lineman. Quarterback Brock Purdy hooked the big dawgs up with brand-new Toyota cars, a video posted by the 49ers on Thursday showed.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Real Salt Lake's 2025 MLS season kicks off Feb. 22 with away game in San Jose
item.description: Real Salt Lake unveiled its entire 2025 MLS Season on Thursday, along with a few crucial dates such as the club's return to the CONCACAF Champions Cup.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Utah HC erases two-goal deficit to stun Vancouver
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Mikhail Sergachev scored with 12 seconds left in overtime, as the Utah Hockey Club stormed back from a 2-0 deficit in the third period to beat the Vanouver Canucks and end a five-game home losing streak, 3-2.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: American competitive eater Joey Chestnut named Holiday Bowl Parade Grand Marshall
item.description: World-famous competitive eater Joey Chestnut has been chosen as the 2024 DIRECTV Holiday Bowl "Chief Eggnog Official." He will also serve as the Grand Marshal of the Holiday Bowl Parade.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Basketball: Santa Fe Christian 63, Coronado 39
item.description: Boys basketball action taking place at Coronado High School as the Islanders take on #4 ranked Santa Fe Christian.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Basketball: La Jolla 55, Army-Navy 47
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys hoops in La Jolla with the Vikings hosting Army-Navy from Carlsbad. Great game that went right down to the wire, La Jolla won 52-47. Vikings were lead by Leo Hawkinson with 16. Warriors were lead by Yanler Ramos game high 26 points.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: Government funding deal opens door to move Washington Commanders stadium to DC
item.description: The Washington Commanders could soon be kicking off in the heart of the nation’s capital. The government funding deal unveiled by congressional negotiators Tuesday paved the way for moving the Commanders' stadium from Maryland to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Gimmicks, not football, dominating college bowl season
item.description: College bowl games are becoming less about football and more about marketing in an ever-changing sports landscape
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Guentzel scores in 7 consecutive games, 1 shy of matching the franchise record in Tuesday's win against Blue Jackets
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Nick Paul had a goal and two assists to lead the Tampa Bay Lightning to a 5-3 victory over the Columbus Blue Jackets. Jake Guentzel, Luke Glendening, Brayden Point and Mitchell Chaffee also scored for Tampa Bay, which has won five of six. Andrei Vasilevskiy finished with 28 saves.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Runnin' Utes roll past Florida A&M, 89-59
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah's last home game of 2024 was a memorable one, as the Utes cruised past Florida A&M, 89-59. Mike Sharavjamts and Mason Madsen each scored 15 points off the bench to lead a balanced Utah attack. The Utes bench scored 57 points, while the starters scored 32.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Donato caps Blackhawks comeback, Blackhawks top Capitals 3-2
item.description: Ryan Donato scored his team-leading 11th goal with 2:18 left in regulation to cap Chicago's three-goal, third-period rally and lift the Blackhawks to a 3-2 win over the Washington Capitals on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cubs trade catcher Matt Thaiss to White Sox for cash considerations
item.description: The White Sox have acquired catcher Matt Thaiss from the Cubs in exchange for cash considerations, adding to their 40-man roster.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: This is when you'll be able to start watching San Diego FC live
item.description: San Diego FC on Tuesday released its full preseason schedule for the 2025 Major League Soccer season.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Gators look to tie a bow on season in Gasparilla Bowl against Tulane
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The Union Home Mortgage Gasparilla Bowl hosts the Tulane Green Wave (9-4) representing the American Athletic Conference and the University of Florida Gators (7-5) from the Southeastern Conference.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Dodgers MVP heading to New York Yankees: report
item.description: The New York Yankees agreed to acquire outfielder Cody Bellinger from the Chicago Cubs on Tuesday for right-hander Cody Poteet, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press. The person spoke to the AP on condition of anonymity because the trade had not been announced.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: NBA unveils new All-Star format featuring four-team tournament
item.description: The NBA 's All-Star Game is going to be an All-Star tournament this season, with the league announcing on Tuesday that it has finalized plans to use a different format for the upcoming midseason showcase in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rays call for more money after Pinellas commissioners vote in favor of bonds for new stadium
item.description: Pinellas County commissioners voted in favor of approving bonds for the new Tampa Bay Rays stadium Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bears offense still sluggish, Vikings win easily and send Chicago to eighth straight loss
item.description: The Chicago Bears lost to the Minnesota Vikings 30-12, marking their eighth consecutive loss and dropping them to 4-10 on the season.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Isaac Wilson decides to return to Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Upon second thought, Isaac Wilson is returning to Utah. Eight days after announcing he was entering the transfer portal, Wilson announced that he is returning to Utah, where will compete for the starting quarterback job with New Mexico transfer Devon Dampier.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Vintage performances by Davante Adams and Aaron Rodgers carry Jets past defenseless Jaguars 32-25
item.description: Davante Adams and Aaron Rodgers gave the New York Jets' next general manager and head coach something to ponder with a glimpse into the past — and potentially the future.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cowboys cornerback Trevon Diggs set for season-ending knee surgery, AP sources say
item.description: Dallas Cowboys cornerback Trevon Diggs is set for season-ending surgery on his left knee to deal with an issue that was about to sideline him for the third time in four games, two people with knowledge of the decision said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5
